Temenia regia is a plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae), native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, providing relief from fever, pain, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Temenia regia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional African medicine, Temenia regia is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in modern herbal remedies in the form of extracts and capsules.
